forecast-period.text: A cold front sweeps across southern SA today. This front will push the inland trough into the far north, exiting the state early Friday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate in the wake of the front as a strong high pressure system moves in from the west from Friday, establishing in the Bight on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon and evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres by early evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Port Neill to Port Victoria during the day. Winds turning southwesterly in the late afternoon.
South to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.
